question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"func \\(n \\*node\\) addRoute|func \\(n \\*node\\) getValue|type node struct|func longestCommonPrefix|func countParams|func cleanPath\" tree.go"
graph.answer:
The runtime spine is:

- Public construction starts in [gin.go:202](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:202) `New` and [gin.go:236](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:236) `Default`.
- `Default` is the convenience path: it calls `New()`, then installs `Logger()` and `Recovery()`, then applies any `OptionFunc`s.
- Route registration flows through [routergroup.go:86](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go:86) `RouterGroup.handle`, [routergroup.go:241](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/routergroup.go:241) `combineHandlers`, and [gin.go:364](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:364) `addRoute`; the trie insertion work happens in [tree.go:135](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go:135) `node.addRoute`.
- Startup goes through [gin.go:540](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:540) `Run` and the other `Run*` variants. They all end up wiring the engine into an `http.Server` through [gin.go:243](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:243) `Handler`.
- Incoming requests enter at [gin.go:662](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:662) `ServeHTTP`.
- `ServeHTTP` does one-time route-tree normalization via [gin.go:517](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:517) `updateRouteTrees`, pulls a pooled `Context`, resets it, and calls [gin.go:690](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:690) `handleHTTPRequest`.
- The actual route match is in [tree.go:418](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/tree.go:418) `node.getValue`. On a hit, `handleHTTPRequest` installs `c.handlers` and `c.fullPath`, then calls [context.go:198](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go:198) `Context.Next`.
- `Context.Next` is the chain executor. `Abort` at [context.go:217](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context.go:217) stops pending handlers.
- Miss paths go through [gin.go:326](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:326) `NoRoute`, [gin.go:332](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:332) `NoMethod`, and [gin.go:764](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:764) `serveError`.
- Re-entry is handled by [gin.go:680](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin.go:680) `HandleContext`, which re-dispatches a rewritten path.

Read next, in this order:

- Constructors and option wiring: [gin_test.go:467](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:467), [gin_test.go:993](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:993)
- Route registration and validation: [gin_test.go:437](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:437), [gin_test.go:455](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:455), [gin_test.go:612](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:612)
- 404 and 405 composition: [gin_test.go:472](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:472), [gin_test.go:494](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:494), [gin_test.go:525](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:525), [gin_test.go:573](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:573), [gin_test.go:1051](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1051)
- The request-entry variants that all converge on the same dispatcher: [gin_test.go:1035](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1035), [gin_test.go:1081](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1081), [gin_test.go:1099](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1099), [gin_test.go:1117](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1117)
- Route-tree update behavior: [gin_test.go:1143](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:1143)
- Re-entry and middleware accounting: [gin_test.go:654](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:654), [gin_test.go:708](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:708), [gin_test.go:746](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:746), [gin_test.go:781](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_test.go:781)
- The handler chain itself: [context_test.go:3555](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/context_test.go:3555)
- End-to-end server startup: [gin_integration_test.go:66](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:66), [gin_integration_test.go:157](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:157), [gin_integration_test.go:210](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:210), [gin_integration_test.go:233](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:233), [gin_integration_test.go:282](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:282), [gin_integration_test.go:392](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/gin/gin_integration_test.go:392)

If you want, I can turn this into a narrower “first behavior change” map for one specific area, like routing, middleware, or server startup.
